mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate environment is accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2020-12-01 indicates deep easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 1.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month employment growth measured by PAYEMS accelerates above 200,000 net new jobs, signaling robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when the trigger is activated.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,624 thousand on 2020-12-01 reflects post-pandemic recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor rebound
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this interaction indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations and predicts positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month change
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,547 thousand on 2020-11-01 reflects housing recovery
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with synchronized consumer and housing activity
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint effect (measured by interaction coefficient) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%, signaling supply-side weakness that constrains corporate earnings and reduces equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the trigger activates.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.72 on 2020-12-01 shows post-pandemic supply recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 (i.e., signal direction reverses) over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating a negative real rate environment. This interaction between inflation and monetary policy signals financial repression that drives equity returns upward over 60 trading days as investors seek real asset protection.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 261.78 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2020-12-01
regime_hint: low-rate inflationary environment with negative real yields
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns in triggered periods do not exceed non-triggered periods by at least 2.0% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and corporate hiring expectations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% on 2020-12-01 reflects pandemic-elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession phase
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any rolling 2-year window when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly accelerates above 3.0% annualized rate, indicating robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during expansion periods.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,596.5 billion (2017 dollars) on 2020-07-01 reflects post-shock recovery
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns during quarters with GDPC1 growth > 3.0% do not exceed baseline by at least 1.2% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously contract, signaling synchronized demand collapse across housing and consumption. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when both demand indicators deteriorate together.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly change < -10% AND RSAFS monthly change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,547 thousand and RSAFS at 540,915 million on 2020-11-01
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp demand contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any rolling 18-month window.
